skill.name: minimax-music-gen
skill.description: Guides users through MiniMax music generation with vocal, instrumental, and cover workflows. It prepares prompts, lyrics, CLI parameters, saved output paths, and playback steps for the mmx tool.
skill.summary: Generate songs, instrumentals, and covers with MiniMax through guided mmx CLI workflows.
skill.author: MiniMax-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported a critical heuristic because Markdown combines command examples, a MiniMax URL, and a credentials path. Manual review found no malicious intent or prompt injection. It did confirm external commands, API credentials, and possible prompt or audio submission to MiniMax. Publish with a medium-risk warning about CLI execution, credential handling, and third-party data transfer.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run mmx, npm, command -v, and local audio player commands. This is expected for the skill purpose, but user-controlled prompts, lyrics, URLs, and file paths must be passed as safe arguments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly tells agents to execute these commands. The risk is real but aligned with the stated music generation workflow.

medium_findings.title: Credential Storage and Remote API Use
medium_findings.description: The skill requires a MiniMax API key, documents storage in a home-directory credentials file, and uses mmx models that call the MiniMax service. This is legitimate for the product, but it exposes users to secret handling and third-party data transfer risks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key command and credentials path are explicit. The network use is inferred from documented MiniMax API model calls through mmx.

medium_findings.title: Reference Audio May Be Uploaded to External Service
medium_findings.description: Cover mode accepts a local audio path or URL and sends it through mmx music cover. Users must avoid private, copyrighted, or unauthorized audio unless they intend to submit it to MiniMax.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cover workflow clearly accepts local files and URLs. The exact transport is delegated to mmx, so impact depends on CLI behavior and user input.

low_findings.title: Static Markdown Pattern Matches Dismissed
low_findings.description: Many static hits are Markdown code fences, inline backticks, prompt examples, or music vocabulary. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptographic algorithm use, system reconnaissance, obfuscation, or prompt injection instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows prose and shell examples rather than hidden code or cryptographic APIs. The files are Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby or JavaScript source.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Execution Required
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs agents to run mmx, npm, command -v, and local audio player commands. This is expected for the skill purpose, but user-controlled prompts, lyrics, URLs, and file paths must be passed as safe arguments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly tells agents to execute these commands. The risk is real but aligned with the stated music generation workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Storage and Remote API Use
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill requires a MiniMax API key, documents storage in a home-directory credentials file, and uses mmx models that call the MiniMax service. This is legitimate for the product, but it exposes users to secret handling and third-party data transfer risks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The API key command and credentials path are explicit. The network use is inferred from documented MiniMax API model calls through mmx.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reference Audio May Be Uploaded to External Service
dangerous_patterns.description: Cover mode accepts a local audio path or URL and sends it through mmx music cover. Users must avoid private, copyrighted, or unauthorized audio unless they intend to submit it to MiniMax.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cover workflow clearly accepts local files and URLs. The exact transport is delegated to mmx, so impact depends on CLI behavior and user input.

content.value_statement: Creating usable music prompts, lyrics, and audio settings can be slow and inconsistent. This skill guides MiniMax song, instrumental, and cover generation through a structured mmx CLI workflow.
actual_capabilities: Builds rich English prompts for MiniMax music generation from short user descriptions.
Supports vocal songs with generated lyrics or user-provided lyrics with section markers.
Supports instrumental tracks with mood, genre, instrument, tempo, and structure guidance.
Supports cover workflows from a local audio file or an audio URL.
Saves generated files to a documented local music folder and can suggest playback commands.
Localizes user-facing interaction while keeping API prompts optimized for generation quality.
limitations: Requires Node.js, the mmx CLI, a MiniMax API key, and network access.
Generated quality, speed, quota, and filtering depend on the MiniMax service.
Cover mode requires user rights to the reference audio and may upload that audio to MiniMax.
It is not intended for existing music playback, music theory lessons, or music recommendations alone.

use_cases.title: Draft a Song Demo
use_cases.description: Turn a theme, mood, and vocal direction into a generated song with editable lyrics and style settings.

use_cases.title: Create Background Audio
use_cases.description: Generate instrumental tracks for videos, prototypes, games, or presentations with specific mood and tempo guidance.

use_cases.title: Explore Cover Arrangements
use_cases.description: Use a reference audio file or URL to create a new stylistic arrangement with original lyrics when needed.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Instrumental Track
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a simple mood or scene.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an instrumental track for a calm morning study session. Use warm piano, soft strings, and a slow tempo.

prompt_templates.title: Vocal Song from Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants a complete song idea with generated lyrics.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a hopeful indie pop song about starting over after a difficult year. Use clear vocals and a bright chorus.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Lyrics and Style Control
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to refine lyrics, structure, and generation parameters before creating audio.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me prepare a full song with verse, chorus, bridge, and outro sections. The style is cinematic R&B at about 90 BPM.

prompt_templates.title: Cover Arrangement Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using source audio and a target style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a cover plan from my reference audio. Make it acoustic, intimate, and slower, with original lyrics in the same theme.

output_examples.input: Make a sad piano instrumental for a rainy city scene.
output_examples.output: The skill prepares a melancholic piano prompt, selects instrumental mode, suggests a slow tempo, and saves the generated track path.

output_examples.input: Turn these lyrics into a pop song with a strong chorus.
output_examples.output: The skill formats lyrics with sections, proposes pop production details, asks for confirmation, and then generates the song.

output_examples.input: Use my audio file and make an acoustic cover.
output_examples.output: The skill asks for the source path, confirms the cover style, warns about rights and privacy, and prepares the cover command.

best_practices: Confirm the prompt, lyrics, source audio, and output path before running generation.
Use original lyrics or properly licensed source material for cover workflows.
Keep API credentials in the mmx authentication flow instead of placing secrets in prompts.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private keys, tokens, or unrelated credentials into generation prompts.
Do not submit private or copyrighted audio unless you have rights and consent.
Do not run generated shell commands without checking paths, URLs, and quoted user input.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate vocal songs, instrumental tracks, and cover arrangements through the MiniMax mmx CLI.

faq.question: Does it require a MiniMax account?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow requires the mmx CLI, a MiniMax API key, and available service quota.

faq.question: Can it write lyrics?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use MiniMax lyric optimization or help format user-provided lyrics with song section markers.

faq.question: Can it create instrumental music?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports instrumental mode with mood, genre, instrument, tempo, and scene guidance.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The skill documents saving generated audio under the local Music/minimax-gen folder.

faq.question: What security care is needed?
faq.answer: Review commands before running them, protect API credentials, and avoid submitting private or unauthorized audio.
